title: As trade war with China looms, how can the EU defend itself?
content: The European Commission is considering a range of tools to shield the bloc from cheap Chinese imports, including reducing its dependence on Chinese components and imposing tariffs on strategic sectors.
snippet: The European Commission is considering a range of tools to shield the bloc from cheap Chinese imports, including reducing its dependence on Chinese components and imposing tariffs on strategic sectors

title: Spain keeps tilting right despite Pedro Sánchez’s progressive international agenda
content: A regional vote in Andalusia points to a rightward shift in Spanish politics, despite Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s international push to position the country at the centre of progressive politics.
snippet: A regional vote in Andalusia points to a rightward shift in Spanish politics, despite Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s international push to position the country at the centre of progressive politics.

title: Amid growing hantavirus worries, how prepared is Europe for new health threats?
content: Although there is still no single European playbook for quarantine measures, the EU has taken steps to be better prepared in the future than it was six years ago. Will it be enough?
snippet: Although there is still no single European playbook for quarantine measures, the EU has taken steps to be better prepared in the future than it was six years ago. Will it be enough?

title: EU honours Merkel as stabilizer in nod to Trump-era turbulence
content: The former German chancellor receives the new European Order of Merit this week. This will likely revive a debate about her legacy - her accomplishments as well as her dark sides.
snippet: The former German chancellor receives the new European Order of Merit this week. This will likely revive a debate about her legacy - her accomplishments as well as her dark sides.

title: ‘A lot of masculinity in the room’: Kallas argues for greater female participation in diplomacy
content: The EU’s top diplomat argues that having more women at the negotiating table during peace talks will result in better outcomes for global conflicts, including Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.
snippet: The EU’s top diplomat argues that having more women at the negotiating table during peace talks will result in better outcomes for global conflicts, including Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.

title: Von der Leyen lauds 'dynamic new era' in EU-India relations as Modi visits Sweden
content: During the second leg of Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's European tour, he visited Sweden's Gothenburg to meet his Swedish counterpart Ulf Kristersson, European Commission Chief Ursula von der Leyen, as well as European and Indian business leaders.
snippet: During the second leg of Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's European tour, he visited Sweden's Gothenburg to meet his Swedish counterpart Ulf Kristersson, European Commission Chief Ursula von der L

title: Terrorism scenario excluded by investigators following car attack in Italy's Modena
content: Italy's Interior Minister Matteo Piantedosi said the 31-year-old man who drove into a crowd in Modena on Saturday was not suspected of a terrorist act and was dealing with mental issues.
snippet: Italy's Interior Minister Matteo Piantedosi said the 31-year-old man who drove into a crowd in Modena on Saturday was not suspected of a terrorist act and was dealing with mental issues.

title: UK leadership hopeful Streeting calls Brexit a 'catastrophic mistake'
content: Streeting has said he will stand in any leadership race to replace British Prime Minister Keir Starmer.
snippet: Streeting has said he will stand in any leadership race to replace British Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

title: One of Ukraine's largest drone attacks kills 3 in Moscow area, Russia says
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy confirms the attack in a post on X, writing that "Ukrainian long-range sanctions reached the Moscow region."
snippet: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy confirms the attack in a post on X, writing that "Ukrainian long-range sanctions reached the Moscow region."

title: Spanish Conservatives on track for majority in Andalusia as Socialists hit record low
content: The PP would retain control of the Andalusian government with 59 seats, while the PSOE would record their worst result to date. Participation at 18:00 was 52,16%, 7,6 points more than in 2022
snippet: The PP would retain control of the Andalusian government with 59 seats, while the PSOE would record their worst result to date. Participation at 18:00 was 52,16%, 7,6 points more than in 2022

title: Several injured after car ploughs into crowd in Italy's Modena
content: A 31-year-old man has been detained after ploughing his car into several people in Modena's city centre. The mayor said at least eight were injured, four seriously.
snippet: A 31-year-old man has been detained after ploughing his car into several people in Modena's city centre. The mayor said at least eight were injured, four seriously.

title: London police out in force as tens of thousands attend rival rallies
content: The Metropolitan Police deployed 4,000 officers alongside horses, dogs, drones and helicopters to manage far-right activist Tommy Robinson's so-called "Unite the Kingdom" march and a rival pro-Palestinian rally marking Nakba Day.
snippet: The Metropolitan Police deployed 4,000 officers alongside horses, dogs, drones and helicopters to manage far-right activist Tommy Robinson's so-called "Unite the Kingdom" march and a rival pro-Palesti

title: Whale Timmy is dead, GPS tracker confirms identity
content: For days, it remained unclear whether the whale found dead off the Danish coast was Timmy. Authorities have now confirmed that the well-known humpback whale did not survive his journey through the Baltic Sea.
snippet: For days, it remained unclear whether the whale found dead off the Danish coast was Timmy. Authorities have now confirmed that the well-known humpback whale did not survive his journey through the Bal

title: Indian PM Modi begins European tour with meeting of Dutch royals
content: Modi's European tour includes a Sunday stop in Sweden, where he will meet Ursula von der Leyen, and will conclude in Italy on May 20 with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni.
snippet: Modi's European tour includes a Sunday stop in Sweden, where he will meet Ursula von der Leyen, and will conclude in Italy on May 20 with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni.

title: French judge opens probe into 2018 killing of Jamal Khashoggi
content: France is to open an inquiry after several NGOs filed a complaint against Saudi crown prince Mohammed bin Salman, accused of ordering the dissident journalist's murder.
snippet: France is to open an inquiry after several NGOs filed a complaint against Saudi crown prince Mohammed bin Salman, accused of ordering the dissident journalist's murder.

title: Andalusia campaign marked by controversy ends ahead of key Sunday vote
content: Spain's most populous region ends a campaign marked by controversy, in elections followed abroad for their potential political impact.
snippet: Spain's most populous region ends a campaign marked by controversy, in elections followed abroad for their potential political impact.

title: Hundreds of Hungarian roads may be contaminated with asbestos
content: Measurements have shown that more than 300 sites in western Hungary have been covered with aggregates containing asbestos. There are currently no preventive regulations in place in Europe regarding natural asbestos.

title: Norway defends move to cancel missile system sale following criticism from Malaysia
content: Norway's foreign ministry said exports of some of Oslo's “most sensitive” defense technologies would be limited to its “allies and closest partners.”
snippet: Norway's foreign ministry said exports of some of Oslo's “most sensitive” defense technologies would be limited to its “allies and closest partners.”

title: Spanish row over EU cash fuels north–south tensions ahead of tough budget talks
content: Spain is at the centre of a political storm after conservative politicians across Europe accused Madrid of using EU money to pay for pensions. Madrid denies it but the damage may be done as tough budget talks get underway.
snippet: Spain is at the centre of a political storm after conservative politicians across Europe accused Madrid of using EU money to pay for pensions. Madrid denies it but the damage may be done as tough budg

title: MEP and far-right influencers barred from UK rally: Who are they and what was the reason?
content: The UK government barred seven individuals from entering the UK, stating that their presence is "not conducive to the public good". But on what grounds and what does this decision mean in practice?
snippet: The UK government barred seven individuals from entering the UK, stating that their presence is "not conducive to the public good". But on what grounds and what does this decision mean in practice?

title: Ex-defence minister admits Latvia faces security 'questions' but no silver bullet to stop drones
content: Latvia faces political turmoil after its prime minister resigned and the defence minister stood down, raising concerns about the country’s readiness to counter potential Russian aggression on Europe’s frontline.
snippet: Latvia faces political turmoil after its prime minister resigned and the defence minister stood down, raising concerns about the country’s readiness to counter potential Russian aggression on Europe’s

title: Poland makes history with first same-sex marriage registration following EU court ruling
content: In November, the European Union’s highest court ordered Poland to register same-sex marriages that were entered into in other EU countries even if Polish law does not currently permit them.
snippet: In November, the European Union’s highest court ordered Poland to register same-sex marriages that were entered into in other EU countries even if Polish law does not currently permit them.

title: Denmark's Queen Margrethe hospitalised in Copenhagen with chest pain, royal court says
content: Hugely popular among Danes for subtly modernising the monarchy, Margrethe has faced multiple health problems in recent years.
snippet: Hugely popular among Danes for subtly modernising the monarchy, Margrethe has faced multiple health problems in recent years.

title: Russian parliament approves law allowing Putin to invade other countries
content: Russia's State Duma approved a law allowing the use of the armed forces “to protect Russian citizens abroad,” which de facto permits Vladimir Putin to invade other countries.
snippet: Russia's State Duma approved a law allowing the use of the armed forces “to protect Russian citizens abroad,” which de facto permits Vladimir Putin to invade other countries.

title: Russia's prison population falls by 40%, partly by sending convicts to fight in Ukraine
content: Russia, which has a massive prison network inherited from Soviet labour camps, has one of the world's largest convict populations, though that number has been decreasing in the last 20 years.
snippet: Russia, which has a massive prison network inherited from Soviet labour camps, has one of the world's largest convict populations, though that number has been decreasing in the last 20 years.

title: Former Google executive Matt Brittin takes reins at scandal-hit public broadcaster BBC
content: Brittin replaces Tim Davie, who had held the BBC post since 2020. He resigned in November over a lawsuit brought by US President Donald Trump.
snippet: Brittin replaces Tim Davie, who had held the BBC post since 2020. He resigned in November over a lawsuit brought by US President Donald Trump.

title: Magyar signals first openness to Ukraine accession talks since Orbán’s departure
content: Hungary's new government says it is open to engaging with Ukraine on its accession to the EU, according to several sources. Meanwhile, a technical dialogue with Kyiv has been announced on the thorny issue of Hungarian minorities. Brussels looks for a breakthrough.

title: Bodies of four divers who drowned in Maldives diving accident found, Italy says
content: Five Italian divers are believed to have died while exploring a cave at a depth of about 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll on Thursday, according to Italy’s Foreign Ministry.
snippet: Five Italian divers are believed to have died while exploring a cave at a depth of about 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll on Thursday, according to Italy’s Foreign Ministry.

title: Gaza-bound aid flotilla says it is 'under attack' as Israel intercepts fleet off Cyprus
content: The Israeli foreign ministry said the aim of the flotilla was to "serve Hamas, to divert attention from Hamas's refusal to disarm and to obstruct progress on President Trump's peace plan."
snippet: The Israeli foreign ministry said the aim of the flotilla was to "serve Hamas, to divert attention from Hamas's refusal to disarm and to obstruct progress on President Trump's peace plan."

title: Russia unleashes another aerial barrage on Ukraine as war's long-range strikes escalate
content: In more than four years of war, Ukraine has built up its own long-range capabilities and has been hitting oil facilities and other targets deep inside Russia.
snippet: In more than four years of war, Ukraine has built up its own long-range capabilities and has been hitting oil facilities and other targets deep inside Russia.

title: Meloni urges EU to treat energy crisis like defence emergency, relax fiscal rules
content: Meloni calls for greater budget flexibility to tackle rising energy costs, warning Italy could exit the SAFE defence programme, in a new letter sent to the European Commission.
snippet: Meloni calls for greater budget flexibility to tackle rising energy costs, warning Italy could exit the SAFE defence programme, in a new letter sent to the European Commission.

title: Zelenskyy's former chief of staff released on €2.7 million bail in anti-corruption probe
content: According to Ukraine's anti-corruption investigators, Andriy Yermak is a suspect in a large money laundering probe tied to the construction of a luxury residence complex outside Kyiv.
snippet: According to Ukraine's anti-corruption investigators, Andriy Yermak is a suspect in a large money laundering probe tied to the construction of a luxury residence complex outside Kyiv.

title: 'We want to create bridges, not burn them,' says Finnish Foreign Trade Minister on EU-US deal
content: Finnish Foreign Trade Minister Ville Tavio expressed “cautious optimism” about the EU-US trade deal, ahead of meetings with fellow EU ministers in Brussels this week. Speaking on Europe Today, he said “our requirement is that the US also respects this deal”.

title: Exclusive: Poland’s hydrogen buses confront prohibitive fuel costs, new report claims
content: The new report argues that despite massive investment in hydrogen-powered buses in Poland, electric buses are less costly and less polluting due to Warsaw's reliance on non-renewable hydrogen. In contrast, Germany emerges as a success story for hydrogen buses.

title: Around 10 'new' victims in France's Epstein probe have come forward, prosecutor says
content: France opened a human trafficking investigation after the US Justice Department released the latest cache of files from the investigation into the disgraced financier in January.
snippet: France opened a human trafficking investigation after the US Justice Department released the latest cache of files from the investigation into the disgraced financier in January.

title: Can the EU stop 'nudification' apps?
content: The EU is banning “nudification” apps, AI-powered tools that can digitally remove clothing from images of people, to create non-consensual sexual content.
snippet: The EU is banning “nudification” apps, AI-powered tools that can digitally remove clothing from images of people, to create non-consensual sexual content.

title: Watch: Brussels & European capitals—how New Delhi is leveraging a two-tier strategy in Europe
content: India's Prime Minister is launching a massive diplomatic offensive across Europe. If we follow Modi's itinerary day by day, the calendar reveals exactly what is at stake.
snippet: India's Prime Minister is launching a massive diplomatic offensive across Europe. If we follow Modi's itinerary day by day, the calendar reveals exactly what is at stake.

title: EU-US trade deal has its make-or-break moment in Strasbourg
content: EU governments and the European Parliament are set to discuss the implementation of the EU-US trade deal again, amid threats of higher tariffs from Trump. As the clock is ticking, this could be the last chance to finalize it before the 4 July deadline.

title: Newsletter: Brexit thrust to centre of UK's Labour leadership showdown

title: Europe Today: Sánchez dealt blow in Andalusia elections — what now for Spain and Brussels?
content: Pedro Sánchez is facing fresh political pressure after his Socialist Party lost regional elections in Andalusia. The result is being closely watched in Madrid and Brussels, with the conservative People’s Party expected to rely on far-right support to govern.

title: Is EU legislation protecting you from AI deepfakes? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot

title: Baby dies of hypothermia as migrants reach Lampedusa overnight
content: A baby girl only a few weeks old was pronounced dead on arrival on the island of Lampedusa after crossing the Mediterranean from Tunisia in a small boat with her mother and sister. An investigation has been opened.

title: Russia launches nearly 300 drones at Ukraine as 528 bodies of Ukrainian soldiers are returned
content: Russian forces launched hundreds of strikes across Ukraine overnight, with direct hits by drones recorded at 15 locationsm Ukraine's Air Force said. Meanwhile, the bodies of 528 Ukrainian soldiers were returned a day after Russia and Ukraine exchanged prisoners of war.

title: Naples: 12 thieves arrested for robbing tourists of luxury watches in Spain
content: Spain's National Police, with Italy and Europol, have arrested in Naples 12 members of a gang that robbed tourists of luxury watches in Marbella, Barcelona and Ibiza.
snippet: Spain's National Police, with Italy and Europol, have arrested in Naples 12 members of a gang that robbed tourists of luxury watches in Marbella, Barcelona and Ibiza.

title: Council of Europe reinterprets migrant rights, Meloni champions 'Italian model'

title: Search continues for bodies of Italian divers who died in Maldives
content: Five Italians died during the incident while reportedly "attempting to explore caves at a depth of 50 metres," the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs said earlier this week.
snippet: Five Italians died during the incident while reportedly "attempting to explore caves at a depth of 50 metres," the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs said earlier this week.

title: How to get Euronews displayed as your favourite news source on Google

title: World leader in Earth observation made in Europe: Small satellites from Finland see everything
content: EU governments are eager to work with ICEYE. The Finnish space company sells mini satellites that help allied nations safeguard their sovereignty. That’s because when it comes to Earth observation and military reconnaissance, the high-resolution radar eyes in space are second to none.

title: Ukraine and Russia swap 205 prisoners of war each in US-brokered exchange
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Telegram most of the Ukrainians handed over had been in Russian captivity since 2022.

title: FC Barcelona tells Euronews 'no comment' after Israel accuses Yamal of 'inciting hatred'
content: In response to a Euronews request for a comment, FC Barcelona declined to back its star player Lamine Yamal after Israel’s defence minister accused him of “inciting hatred towards Israel” for waving a Palestinian flag. The club said it had “no comment” to make on the issue.

title: Five countries push back against Commission's electric grid plans
content: The five capitals argue that the European Commission oversteps member states’ national competences and misunderstands how energy systems operate. They call for a European Union based on coordinated regional systems rather than a fully centralised energy union.

title: Could the EU’s next budget shrink civil society and NGOs?
content: The European Commission says its next long-term budget will strengthen democratic values. Civil society groups warn it could instead quietly weaken NGOs by removing protected funding and shifting control to national governments.
